title: Restore a commit found in the reflog
description: Find a dangling commit in git reflog and reattach it to a branch so the work becomes reachable again.
body: Use when a reset, aborted rebase, or branch deletion left a commit unreferenced. Do not run aggressive garbage collection while the commit is still missing. Actions: run git reflog and read entries for HEAD or the affected branch; identify the hash; inspect it with git show <hash>; recover with git branch recovered <hash> or git cherry-pick <hash>; verify the content. Validation signals include the recovered commit appearing in git log of a real branch and its diff matching the lost work. Risk boundary: reflog entries are pruned over time, so act before gc removes them.
